answer:
**In *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, Chapter 1 (the work's only chapter), the doctrine of the Five Tathāgatas—Vairocana, Akṣobhya, Ratnasambhava, Amitābha, and Amoghasiddhi—is referenced briefly earlier (e.g., their "Lords" departing if the head is touched improperly during moral conduct instructions), but first systematically articulated in the work's extended final section on esoteric correspondences.**[^1]

This begins explicitly with their mapping to the five aggregates (*skandhas*), followed by their seed-syllables (*bīja*), embodiment of the three poisons (*trimala*), elements (*dhātu*), embryonic stages (*rūpaskandha*), and culminating in their five wisdoms (*pañcajñāna*): eternal (*śāśvatajñāna*, Vairocana), mirror-like (*ādarśajñāna*, Akṣobhya), equality (*samatajñāna*, Ratnasambhava), discriminating (*pratyavekṣaṇajñāna*, Amitābha), and all-accomplishing (*kṛtyānuṣṭhānajñāna*, Amoghasiddhi).[^2][^3][^4][^5]

The chapter's wiki card and TextRank identify the wisdoms introduction as the core, signature passage.[^6]

[^1]: Mpu Shri Sambhara Surya Warama, *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, [p-638b90](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-638b90).
[^2]: *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, [p-88df1a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-88df1a) (five aggregates).
[^3]: *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, [p-204881](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-204881) (seed-syllables).
[^4]: *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, [p-e30d70](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-e30d70) (three poisons).
[^5]: *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, [p-36988a](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/01/translation/#p-36988a) (five wisdoms).
[^6]: *San Hyan Kamahāyānīkan*, Chapter 1, [wiki card](/works/unknown-san-hyan-kamahayanikan-2a0c19/wiki/01.card.md).
